top_signals.title: Eni, Mercuria to Form Global Energy Commodities Trading Venture
top_signals.summary: Italian oil company Eni SpA and commodities merchant Mercuria Energy Group Ltd. signed a deal to trade energy together globally.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: The Defence Investment Plan
top_signals.summary: Equipping our forces, defending our future.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Galderma Shares Slump After FDA Turns Down Botox Rival Relfydess
top_signals.summary: Galderma Group AG shares slumped the most in more than a year after the Swiss dermatology firm said the US Food and Drug Administration had turned down its rival Botox treatment Relfydess.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Industrial firms warn EU carbon overhaul could benefit polluters
top_signals.summary: Now policymakers are facing a dilemma over whether to hold the line on carbon pricing or yield to political pressure and help heavy polluters.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Isabel Schnabel: Interview with Die Zeit
Million-dollar homes, luxury bags and Lamborghinis: Florida man pleads guilty in crypto fraud scheme
top_signals.summary: A man pleaded guilty to fraud charges after prosecutors said he tricked people into the promise of making returns in crypto liquidity pools.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: China's Energy Future Runs on Coal and Solar
top_signals.summary: China will continue spending heavily on both coal and alternative energy, such as wind and solar, over the next five years, Beijing’s latest five-year plan revealed this month. The apparent paradox sees the world’s largest emitter by far continue to generate a solid portion of its energy from coal while also boosting the largest alternative energy capacity in the world. The thing is, it is not actually a paradox. Ever since the signing of the Paris Agreement in 2015, a host of countries have been working hard to reduce their reliance…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
